flow_points: Canada started poorly, losing 3 wickets for 39 runs in the Powerplay.
Junaid Siddique's crucial spell of 5 wickets for 35 runs significantly restricted Canada's innings.
United Arab Emirates had a more stable Powerplay, scoring 39 runs for 1 wicket.
A Sharma anchored United Arab Emirates' innings with 74 runs off 57 balls, contributing 49.0% of their total.
Saad Bin Zafar's spell of 3 wickets for 14 runs created defensive pressure for United Arab Emirates.
Canada's Ansh Patel (economy 8.33) and Jaskaran Singh (economy 12.27) had expensive outings, struggling significantly above their career averages.
United Arab Emirates achieved a high-scoring finish in the Death Overs with a run rate of 13.5, shifting match momentum decisively.
United Arab Emirates won by 5 wickets.
